sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Nice have struggled recently, winning only 1 of their last 5 matches, with a 2-1 victory over Saint-Étienne as their highlight, but suffering 3 losses. RC Strasbourg, on the other hand, are in better shape, remaining unbeaten in their last 4 matches, including wins against USL Dunkerque (2-1) and Breidablik Kópavogur (3-1). This gives Strasbourg a clear edge in momentum heading into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Nice have had a slight advantage, winning 2-1 at home in November 2024 and drawing 2-2 away in April 2025. Both matches saw goals from both sides, suggesting a trend of competitive, high-scoring games between these teams.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Nice, sitting 13th with 17 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 29 goals (16th in the league) while scoring 19. Strasbourg, positioned 7th with 23 points, have a more balanced record with 25 goals scored and 20 conceded, showing greater attacking potency and defensive stability.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Nice will miss several key players like Jérémie Boga and Hicham Boudaoui, potentially impacting their midfield creativity. Strasbourg’s Joaquin Panichelli, with 9 goals this season (2nd in the league), could be a game-changer, especially against Nice’s leaky defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Despite playing at home, Nice’s poor form and defensive issues make them underdogs against a confident Strasbourg side. Sofascore’s prediction of a 47% chance of an away win aligns with current trends, though Nice’s historical edge in head-to-heads could make it close.
